summary: The main claim is: It will come up with a better solution, but the problem can be solved in principle.
Doomer: The AI is smart enough to figure out a solution like nanotechnology or something.
The main claim is: What if the agent can access the ideal belief distribution?
The main claim is: Abhishek Gupta, founder of the Montreal AI Ethics Institute.

Join us this April 15th for a panel discussion, keynote address by the founder of the Montreal AI Ethics Institute, and networking mixer on the governance of AI Systems in the Canadian context, held by EA Carleton and EA Canada.

The main claim is: If using the steg tokens in (B) is able to outperform (A), then the model is able to do "extra thinking" in those steg-token slots (this is computationally possible since the query at position N is able to see the keys of all positions bef…

The main claim is: Because brute-force specification of a concept like 'apple' would require astronomically many bits yet toddlers learn it from a few examples, nearly all the work of learning word meanings must be done by unsupervised priors rather than labeled examples.
Diverse minds converge on the same 'natural abstractions,' which is why humans — and even dogs or neural nets — can coordinate on roughly the same word meanings.
The main claim is: I wonder about a scenario where the first AI with human or superior capabilities would be nothing goal-oriented, eg a language model like GPT.
Then one instance of it would be used, possibly by a random user, to make a conversational agent told to behave as a goal-oriented AI.
The main claim is: Now the question is, is the Orthogonality Thesis at least approximately true in humans?
Basically, the Orthogonality Thesis is arguing that intelligence and goals do not correlate, at least in the limit of infinite compute and data.
The main claim is: Failing to do so would probably bar the main agent from performing this kind of fine tampering with physical reality, and thus greatly limit its capabilities.
These systems with local knowledge will have to maximize a certain state of the world that the main agent wants to attain.
The main claim is: --- I think BIG-bench could be the final AI benchmark: if a language model surpasses the top human score on it, the model is an AGI.
